No deadline day drama at Liverpool - but Klopp and FSG remain united, writes @JamesPearceEcho

Speculation that the club were on the brink of buying Riyad Mahrez was so misplaced that Liverpool took the unusual step of contacting Leicester City to inform them they had no interest in the player. (James Pearce)

Liverpool tried and failed to convince RB Leipzig to bring forward Naby Keita’s summer switch to Anfield. They considered re-igniting their pursuit of Thomas Lemar but were put off by Monaco’s £90million asking price for the France international. (James Pearce)

Some will argue that he’s merely covering for FSG but Klopp is nobody’s patsy. He’s passionate and he speaks from the heart. If he felt he wasn’t being given the tools to do the job to the best of his ability it wouldn’t remain a secret for long. (James Pearce)

Jürgen Klopp admitted it is very difficult to select a Liverpool XI without Roberto Firmino after the forward bagged his 19th goal of the season at Huddersfield Town. (@LFC)

Klopp: "Roberto delivers, delivers and delivers. He doesn’t score all the time, he is not always brilliant, but if he is not brilliant then he works like a horse. It’s very important that you have this kind of stability there."

Klopp: “Ingsy is in a very good way, Dom (Solanke) is in a very good way. But it is not easy to pass Roberto."

Klopp: “Of course, there will be games when he cannot play, that’s absolutely OK. He played quite often, that means most of the time I think, ‘we should try it again with Roberto’."

【now.com體育】儘管李斯特城中場馬列斯入紙要求轉會，但最終還是未能如願加盟曼城，其好友在轉會窗關閉後，發聲明道出該阿爾及利亞球星的失望之情。

馬列斯（Riyad Mahrez）一名好友透過英國《天空體育》發聲明：「經過過去兩天發生的事，馬列斯感到非常沮喪，他不明白李斯特城為何會這樣對待他。剛過去的1月轉會窗，是李斯特城說會讓其離隊的第4個轉會窗，他強烈感到自己已為球會付出一切。」

「他首季效力時，為李斯特城升班貢獻良多，之後幫助他們創這歷史，贏得英超冠軍，他亦已做到了班主對他的要求，就是留在王權球場協助球隊護級成功。他們已讓尼高路簡迪和真禾特離隊，只要他們接受曼城破紀錄的轉會費，馬列斯就將會成為球隊史上出售最高價的球星。以我計算，他們可從當年35萬鎊的馬列斯身上，賺取5980萬鎊。」

「很多前隊友也告訴他要離開李斯特城，並必須為此與球會爭鬥，但他一直嘗試避免發生，他今季為李斯特城全力比賽，因此他應該可在這個轉會窗離隊。加盟曼城是其夢想，為哥迪奧拿效力仍然是他極渴望達成的事，但現在似乎非常遙遠，對於自己那樣努力為球會效力，最終卻遭到這樣對待，他感到非常沮喪。」

Jurgen Klopp: Why Liverpool were right not to panic buy in January - @JamesPearceEcho

Jurgen Klopp says Liverpool stayed “cool” in the January transfer window because it would have taken “completely crazy money” to land one of his attacking targets. [@JamesPearceEcho]

Klopp: “Unfortunately our business is only from the outside that easy. You miss a player for a week and you think ‘okay, we need another player.’”

Klopp: “Yes, we could have done something. But we need really the right thing. The right thing now, you don’t get. The 100% right thing, only for completely crazy money that really makes no sense. I’m not even sure that would work. And in the summer it looks different.”

Klopp: “That’s how it is. To do something just to have any solution that makes no real sense, to be honest.”

Klopp: “People obviously ask ‘bring him in’ because someone is injured and you need somebody else, but then the other one is coming again and you weaken both or the third one, it makes no sense.”

Klopp: “In this situation, you really need to stay cool. It’s more difficult when you’ve lost two games but you need to be serious still.”
